observations.recommendation: 3. We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation, that for future receipt of drugs, medicines, medical supplies and medical equipment from DOH and PHO, the MHO notify and furnish the GSO and Accounting Office with PTR of the received items or any equivalent document before its distribution, for proper accounting thereof. For the unrecorded transactions, the Accounting Office and GSO shall record the items received based on the PTR submitted by the MHO, for a fair and reliable presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management
observations.reason: Reiterated with modification as Observation No. 3 in Part II of this year’s report.

observations.recommendation:
4. Instruct the Municipal GSO to ensure complete submission of the RPCPPE, following the prescribed format for RPCPPE (Annex 49) in MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II. Likewise, ensure that the RPCPPE for each type of PPE are reconciled with the accounting records.

5. Conduct the immediate disposal of the unserviceable PPE amounting to ₱9,876,221.47 in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445 and instruct the Municipal GSO to accomplish the IIRUP which will be the basis for the derecognition of the unserviceable PPE from the books of accounts.

6.  Submit the general/subsidiary ledgers and the lapsing schedule of the PPE to determine the accuracy of the balances of the accounts presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The Management submitted the RPCPPE but not in the prescribed format and not reconciled with the accounting records.

Unserviceable PPE remained not disposed as at year-end.

The Management adhered with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: 7. We recommended that the Management adhere to the disclosure requirements for the payables accounts in the Notes to the Financial Statements, in compliance with Section 80, Chapter 4 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs, Volume I, for the fair presentation thereof in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management

observations.recommendation: 8. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the amounts presented in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts reconciles with the corresponding component of the financial statements. Likewise, the Reconciliation of Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts and the Statement of Financial Performance should be disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements to ensure the accuracy of the balances presented therein.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts and the Statement of Financial Performance were not disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: 10. We recommended that the Management implement the projects as planned in order to maximize the utilization of the projects and to ultimately achieve the socio-economic developments and environmental outcomes of the Municipality. We also recommend that the Management identify and immediately address the causes of delay to avoid the late implementations of projects.
observations.managementAction: Seven projects funded out of the CY 2022 20% DF were not implemented as at year-end.
observations.reason: Reiterated with modification as Observation No. 8 in Part II of this year’s report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to record the reversion of stale checks in the General Fund amounting to ₱127,168.17 pursuant to Section 97 of PD 1445.

Proposed Entries:
For stale checks under General Fund over 6 months but not over 2 years
Cash in Bank – LCCA		       ₱ 127,168.17
Accounts Payable                                 	       ₱ 127,168.17

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to record the understatement of the Real Property Tax Receivable, Special Education Tax Receivable, Deferred Real Property Tax and Deferred Special Education Tax in the amount of ₱1,458,820.46 to arrive at the correct balances thereof and ensure fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

Proposed Entry:
Real Property Tax Receivable		₱1,458,820.46
Special Education Tax Receivable		₱1,458,820.46
Deferred Real Property Tax		                      ₱1,458,820.46		Deferred Special Education Tax   		         ₱1,458,820.46
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to henceforth furnish the Municipal Accountant with a duly certified list showing the names of the taxpayers and the amount collectibles from them to ensure correct amount to be set-up as RPT and SET Receivable at the beginning of the year and likewise as basis in the preparation of subsidiary ledgers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all departments and offices to coordinate with the Municipal Accounting Office for all donations received for proper recording and accounting thereof, in compliance with Paragraphs 96 and 97 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 23 and Revised Chart of Accounts.

We recommended that Management instruct the accountant to reconcile the discrepancies noted between the reported balances of Office Equipment, Information and Communication Technology Equipment, Other Machinery and Equipment and Furniture and Fixture accounts and the subsidiary ledger/supporting schedule amounting to P989,216.00, P126,897.00, P11,0000.00 and P238,945.00, respectively, to ensure reliability of the said accounts.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger/supporting schedule to support the Due to NGAs account to ensure reliability and correctness thereof. Likewise, ensure that details of the Due to NGAs account are adequately disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements, to ensure fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of Accomplishment Reports as supporting document for the payment of wages of Job Order personnel, in compliance with Item 1.2.1 COA Circular No. 2012-001, to ensure propriety of the disbursements.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to improve further its collection efficiency in order to meet or even exceed the BLGF – approved estimates or targets pursuant to Section 256 of the Local Government Code of the Philippines.

We recommended that Management identify and immediately address the causes of delay in the implementation of the seven projects and henceforth ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are carefully planned and procurement and implementation ready, in order to maximize the utilization of the projects and ensure timely delivery of the socio-economic developments and intended benefits to its constituents.

We recommended that Management require the concerned contractors to rectify the defects the soonest time to avoid further deterioration and to ensure longer life span of the structures. Otherwise, the corresponding amounts of deficiencies noted of ₱11,333.28 will be disallowed in audit. Likewise, impose liquidated damages against the contractors and require the refund of the amount equivalent to the 42 days delay in the completion of the three projects.

1. We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to record the reversion of stale checks in the General Fund, Special Education Fund and Trust Fund amounting to ₱1,062,170.91, ₱24,540.90 and ₱3,000.00, respectively, pursuant to Section 97 of PD 1445 and Section 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhered with the audit recommendation

observations.recommendation: 2. We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant that for future procurement, inventories shall be coursed thru the inventory account and correspondingly, issuances thereof shall be recorded to the appropriate expense account.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhered with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: 9. We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries for the reclassification of the Construction in Progress - Infrastructures Assets and Construction in Progress –Buildings and Other Structures to Road Networks and Building and Other Structures in the amount of ₱74,734,758.43 and ₱57,084,109.20, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhered with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: 11. We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to review the unexpended balances pertaining to CY 2021 20% Development Fund appropriation in the amount of ₱2,628,050.53 and revert the same for appropriation to other development projects.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhered with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management adhere to the disclosure requirements as stated in the Section 380 of the GAM for Local Government Units Volume I for the fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management
observations.reason: Reiterated with modification as Observation No. 05 in Part II of this year’s report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit the Reports on the Receipt and Utilization of the DRRMF sourced from NDRRMF under the FY 2020 GAA and other Cash Donations to the Office of the Civil Defense, and copy furnish the Audit Team.

We also recommended compliance to the provisions of COA Circular 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 to avoid delay in the review of the receipt and utilization of cash donations and the communication of audit results.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management advice the Accounting Office and the Budget Office to reconcile the difference between the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts and the Statement of Financial Performance.

We also recommended adherence to the disclosure requirements, particularly the SCBAA, as stated in the Section 380 of the GAM for Local Government Units Volume I for the fair presentation of the financial statements. The Reconciliation Statement Format is found in Annex 8 of LGAM Volume 1.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management.
observations.reason: Reiterated with modification as Observation No. 06 in Part II of this year’s report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that for future similar transactions, the Municipal Health Office notify the General Services Office (GSO) of the receipt and inspection of the goods, before distribution.  The GSO shall likewise furnish the accounting department for the necessary recording of the receipt and issuance in the books of accounts. For the unrecorded transactions, the Accounting Office shall make the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts to correct the affected accounts and for a fair and reliable financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management
observations.reason: Reiterated with modification as Observation No. 03 in Part II of this year’s report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management exert all available remedies in enforcing the immediate settlement of the outstanding suspension and disallowances from the persons responsible and liable respectively, in accordance with COA Circular 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: The Management continuously deducting amount for settlement of disallowance from the salaries of the persons liable currently connected with the Municipality.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant that for future procurement, inventories shall be coursed thru the inventory account and correspondingly, issuances thereof shall be recorded to the appropriate expense account.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation, that for future receipt of drugs, medicines, medical supplies and medical equipment from DOH and PHO, the MHO notify and furnish the GSO and Accounting Office with PTR of the received items or any equivalent document before its distribution, for proper accounting thereof. For the unrecorded transactions, the Accounting Office and GSO shall record the items received based on the PTR submitted by the MHO, for a fair and reliable presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal GSO to ensure complete submission of the RPCPPE, following the prescribed format for RPCPPE (Annex 49) in MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II. Likewise, ensure that the RPCPPE for each type of PPE are reconciled with the accounting records.
Conduct the immediate disposal of the unserviceable PPE amounting to ₱9,876,221.47 in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445 and instruct the Municipal GSO to accomplish the IIRUP which will be the basis for the derecognition of the unserviceable PPE from the books of accounts.
Submit the general/subsidiary ledgers and the lapsing schedule of the PPE to determine the accuracy of the balances of the accounts presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management adhere to the disclosure requirements for the payables accounts in the Notes to the Financial Statements, in compliance with Section 80, Chapter 4 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs, Volume I, for the fair presentation thereof in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the amounts presented in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts reconciles with the corresponding component of the financial statements. Likewise, the Reconciliation of Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts and the Statement of Financial Performance should be disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements to ensure the accuracy of the balances presented therein.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries for the reclassification of the Construction in Progress - Infrastructures Assets and Construction in Progress –Buildings and Other Structures to Road Networks and Building and Other Structures in the amount of ₱74,734,758.43 and ₱57,084,109.20, respectively.

We recommended that the Management implement the projects as planned in order to maximize the utilization of the projects and to ultimately achieve the socio-economic developments and environmental outcomes of the Municipality. We also recommend that the Management identify and immediately address the causes of delay to avoid the late implementations of projects.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to review the unexpended balances pertaining to CY 2021 20% Development Fund appropriation in the amount of ₱2,628,050.53 and revert the same for appropriation to other development projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the accounting records with the records of the  General Services Office, for the proper recording of inventory and expenses as of year-end, and/or adjust their records for the fair presentation of the affected financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reconciled the records with the Municipal General Services Officer for the recording of inventory and expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management depreciate their Property, Plant and Equipment under the Special Education Fund in accordance with Items b and c of Section 262 of the Government Accounting Manual for Local Government Units, Volume I, to present fairly the amount of net income and net book value of the PPEs of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant depreciated their Property, Plant and Equipment under Special Education Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management coordinate with the Department of Education for the proper recognition and derecognition of the school buildings account in the DepEd and LGU books, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The Management conducted inspection of the school buildings and found that the said buildings are dilapidated and the Management decided to derecognized the School Buildings.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management take up the grants as Other Receivables subject for liquidation, as a control in the grant of financial assistance, and for easy monitoring and evaluation of their utilization.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant send Demand Letters to all payees for the submission of Liquidation Reports and documents. Copies of the duly received Demand Letters should be furnished to this Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Management recorded the grants as Other Receivables subject for liquidation and the Municipal Accountant issued Demand Letters to all payees for the submission of Liquidation Reports and documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly follow the computation of employer share as provided under Rule VI, Section I of the IRR of R.A. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009, and stop the payment of excessive contribution.
observations.managementAction: The Management  complied the computation of employer share as provided under Rule VI, Section I of the IRR of R.A. 9679

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management strictly adhere with the aforementioned DILG Opinion and stop contracting job order to personnel who are either elective or appointive public officer or employee.

We also recommended that management  revisit their policies in hiring Job Order Personnel, and align the same  with existing   laws, rules and regulations.
observations.reason: (The Audit Team issued Notice of Disallowances No. 21-001-101-(2020) to 21-041-101-(2020) all dated October 13, 2021

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit their justification for the non-compliance with the posting requirements of the GPPB Circular and Advisories. We also recommended that for the current and future transactions of the LGU, the Management should faithfully conform with the requirements of all Regulatory Bodies to achieve good governance.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted their justification for the non-compliance with the posting requirements of the GPPB Circular and Advisories.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer review the Statement of Comparison and Actual Amounts, and Status of Appropriation, Allotment, Obligation and Balances, and examine the reason of the non–inclusion of the BGCM Fund Receipt and Utilization in their reports. The affected financial statements and reports should be corrected/revised to avoid the issuance of an adverse opinion by the Audit Team in the CY 2020 Financial Statements of the LGU.

It was also required that the quarterly Fund Receipt and Utilization Report, as well as the subsidiary ledger, be submitted immediately.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer revised the Statement of Comparison and Actual Amounts, and Status of Appropriation, Allotment, Obligation and Balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accounting Office immediately submit the Disbursements Vouchers listed in Annex A for the immediate audit of the same and for the prompt communication of audit results, and to avoid the issuance of Notices of Suspension (NS) that may eventually lead to the issuance of Notices of Disallowance (ND).
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office submitted the Disbursements Vouchers.
